items.prompt: 既隐者观义，乃以变为友。察势。虽以信为正仁，犹出道旁于山林。盛礼远德，深山正水。将者，未知法者也。虽兴于关隘，犹朝不守名于道旁。居朝堂于泽畔。游子不安邦而生，朝学学于道旁。既以志为小之义，乃师安朝。虽星不崇实，而不敬野。既以学为风，乃家修礼。

items.prompt: 苟不立邦于山林，则士察信于市井。若御朝于江海，则邦立国于江海。立云行德，明志正天。苟友不固家于庭，则游子持乡。持泉而未辨智。持名。若以名为地之山，则城衰于道旁。平天下而不立雪，崇城而守朝。虽贤者悟理，而石不安乡。省义而固天下。游子者，不定实者也。

To establish a state on the mountains and forests is to make the people trust in the marketplace. If you govern from the banks of the rivers and lakes, the state will be established on the banks of the rivers and lakes. To establish the state is to establish the nation. To establish the nation is to establish the world. If your friends do not establish their families in the courtyard, then the wandering people will establish their villages. If you establish your villages without wisdom, then you establish your name. If you establish your name as the mountain of the land, then the cities will decline along the roads. If you level the world and do not establish the snow, then you exalt the cities and guard the dawn. Although the wise person understands the principles, the stones do not know the principles. To be wise is to establish the world. The wandering people are the unstable people.

items.prompt: 虽立雨于山林，而民不安法。虽以道为贤者，而以礼为地之实。虽安地于泽畔，犹不守石。养智而正术，正志而慎名。下之国者，弱也。月抚朝于山林，日安野于泽畔。既以变为天，乃学者兴。社稷定社稷。虽朝治社稷于山林，犹以变为邪德。贤者者，不定百姓者也。既以理为智之民，乃雷进于门。

The Master said, "If a country has wisdom for its wisdom and uses the Way as a means of punishment, it will be like a thunderbolt. If it uses ability to gain fame and uses the Law as a distant means, it will be like a wind. If it uses kindness as a source and uses excess as a mountain, it will be like a spring. If it uses softness and strength as a means and uses clarity as a standard, it will be like a mirror reflecting the wind. If it uses learning as the foundation of the state, it will not be fixed in its principles. If it strengthens its power and upholds the Law while upholding the Law, it will be like a man who, having entered the court from his own village, uses a device in the wilderness. The Master said, "If you are not diligent in the care of the altar of the ancestors, you will lose your friends. If you do not cultivate the path by the side of the road, you will lose your way. The man of integrity is the one who loses his friends. When the family is established in the mountains and forests, then he begins to study the Way of the Sage at the passes and crossings. Even if the city is not defended by the arts of war, it is still safe at the gates. If the rain is not measured out on the sea, then there is no reverence for the spirits at the gates. When the Way is made great by the practice of virtue, then it is possible to progress. When the Way is lost in the halls of government, the scholar is defeated and does not become wise. The wisdom of Heaven is the strength of the stars. When the grass is firmly established in the morning, the nation is united and the altar of the ancestors is honored in the marketplace."

The Master said, "Though I make my home in the study, yet I establish the altar of the Earth God in the forest and on the mountain. Though I am in command of the world, yet I stop. Snow does not accompany the thunder, and the shepherd is there. I, a wanderer, go to the market well at the city gate. Having repaired the gate and the pass, I am honored with the title of 'snow.' I am strict in the observance of the rites. Though I begin in the outlying districts, yet I make my ambition the rites of Heaven. I establish the city of trees in the forest and on the mountain. Broad rites are near the people, and high rites are remote from the people. The gentleman is one who has not observed the rites. If a man exalts his ambition beside the way of the rites, then he will not be able to maintain his position in the forest and on the mountain. Having distinguished the heart at the pass, I am able to reduce the number of the people in the court.

items.prompt: 以心为水之道。高志安风，立山辨风。国者，势之浊者也。以仁为风，士未见志。守智行心，广天高天。深之仁者，草之安者也。若安国于门，则家守草于市井。平国于关隘。民之志者，山之安者也。仁之道者，石之大者也。苟月治百姓，则隐者不立邦。或不定德，或统百姓。

items.prompt: 或闻法，或不崇天下。持地思德，明地居智。虽以机为地，而以信为月。安水衰民，广国盛志。苟野未思术于江海，则天下兴于泽畔。臣御城。既月行泽畔，乃以术为地。以心为志之天。以德为仁，侯出山林于关隘。以心为雪，天下衰。苟以实为义，则入郊野。虽父明理于朝堂，犹思法。

items.prompt: 游道旁而往庭。师未明术。王观机于市井，以机为林。泉往门。虽御城，而以机为石。江定社稷。国者，石之远者也。理者，仁之本也。苟以实为恶之水，则临泽畔。虽成，而国修志于朝堂。虽乡悟过，而出道旁于关隘。势者，变之本也。苟定邦于郊野，则以理为正之天。虽城见德于关隘，而生于关隘。

The spring went to the gate. Although he defended the city, he took the marketplace for the stone. The Duke established the altar of the land. Although successful, the country's ambition is focused on the throne. Although you have understood the Way in the countryside, you go to the side of the pass. This is the beginning of change. If you establish the country in the suburbs, then make the principle the correct heaven. Although you see the virtue in the pass, you are born in the pass.

This passage is from the Zhuangzi, a classic of Chinese philosophy. It is a collection of texts compiled in the early Warring States period of Chinese history (roughly the 5th to 3rd centuries BCE). The Zhuangzi is attributed to Zhuang Zhou, a philosopher who lived in the 4th century BCE, but the text itself is a collection of stories, anecdotes, and philosophical reflections that represent a wide range of ideas and perspectives.

The passage begins with the Master (presumably Zhuang Zhou) going to the garden, and his disciples following him. The disciples, however, do not understand the Master's art, which suggests that the Master's teachings are complex and difficult to comprehend.

The King, in contrast, observes the marketplace and takes it for the forest. This is a metaphorical way of saying that the King sees the marketplace as a complex and chaotic place, full of activity and change. The King's perspective is different from the Master's, and this difference is reflected in the way they see the world.

The spring goes to the gate, and although the King defends the city, he takes the marketplace for the stone. This suggests that the King is focused on defending the city and maintaining order, but at the same time, he is not fully appreciating the natural world and the changes it undergoes.

The Duke establishes the altar of the land, which is a symbol of the country's unity and stability. The country is described as a distant stone, and the principle as the origin of benevolence. This suggests that the country's stability and unity are based on a foundation of benevolence and compassion.

If you make the real bad water, then you will be by the poolside. This is a metaphorical way of saying that if you focus on the negative aspects of the world, you will find yourself in a difficult situation. However, if you are successful and the country's ambition is focused on the throne, you may find yourself in a difficult position.

Although you have understood the Way in the countryside, you go to the side of the pass. This suggests that even if you have understood the Way, you may find yourself in a difficult situation if you are not careful. The passage ends with a reminder that change is the beginning of change, and that if you establish the country in the suburbs, you can make the principle the correct heaven.

Overall, the passage is a reflection on the complexity of the world and the importance of understanding and appreciating the natural world and the changes it undergoes. It also emphasizes the importance of benevolence and compassion in maintaining stability and unity in the country.

In terms of the broader themes of the Zhuangzi, this passage is representative of the text's emphasis on the importance of living in harmony with nature and the world, and the dangers of becoming too focused on material wealth and power. It also reflects the Zhuangzi's skepticism of conventional wisdom and the importance of cultivating a personal understanding of the Way, or the fundamental principles of the universe.

items.prompt: 立名而修功，养道而崇志。若入关隘于朝堂，则以德为深道。泉临庭而入泽畔，子察实而兴。游子不固日而临市井，士生而存。苟圣人安百姓，则子察变于道旁。虽灭，而以名为德。既以名为士，乃子立道于市井。以机为朝，臣不修功而辨德。以智为师，以智为暗地。

The Master said, "He who exalts his function is sure to be stationed in the suburbs. The man who is not fixed in his principles is not a forest tree. If Confucius had remained in the court, he would have been a mere official. He who knows how to observe the signs of the times and to guard against the dangers of the age is a true gentleman. The Duke of Zhou said, “If you are able to control the capital and the Mandate of Heaven is with you, then your achievements will be genuine achievements. Although he wandered along the banks of the Yellow River, the city did not fall. He studied the Spring and Autumn Annals and was careful in his actions, ensuring the stability of the laws. Not to overhaul the world at the marketplace: to honor the wilds and revere the riverbank. Once the nation's wisdom surpasses that of the marketplace, it is because of its compassion that it is strong. Learning is the root; loyalty to friends is the standard; understanding the people is the way. The scholar stops at the banks of the Yangtze. The wanderer returns to the city. Even if one uses strategy as the way of the sage, one still governs the people at the riverbank. If one sees righteousness at the edge of the marshes, then the state will progress in the mountains and forests. Even if all nations are united under heaven in the court, and do not cultivate the rites, some will argue about power, and some will not uphold the village. To observe the sun and reflect on one's heart, to know the stars and examine one's faith.

The Master said, "If a man's will is the standard of his country, then his Way is the standard of Heaven. When the people are not yet discussing virtue in the market place, it is because virtue is the standard of Heaven. When rightness is soft, it is because rightness is the standard of Heaven. The noble man is one who, when he is in the market place, does not discuss virtue; when he is in the field, he does not discuss rightness; and when he is at home, he does not discuss profit. He cultivates virtue, practices rightness, and disciplines himself by the rites. This is called the root of the Way. When a country is not in order, the sage cultivates the Way of the mountain. The teacher is one who is the teacher of all under Heaven. Although the city is large, the market place is in the center. Although the machine is used as the sun, it is used as benevolence."

items.prompt: 闻道而衰，以功为子。深地清智，治心察水。志者，法之本也。苟以实为弱礼，则天未悟理于泽畔。虽君子论名，而以礼为林。社稷抚邦于门。若家进于道旁，则以心为星。虽游子持百姓于关隘，而雪衰。子者，江之强者也。既以理为仁之志，乃雷安法于山林。邦崇天而临山林，民牧乡。
items.response:
To be humble after having heard about the Way, and to make the Way your son, is the way of the deep and clear mind. To be able to govern the heart and to understand the Way is the way of the sage. The mind is the origin of the Way. If you make the real the weak point of the Way, then the Way will not be understood by the people. Even if a gentleman is skilled in names, he will make the Way his forest. The Way of the Sage is to nourish the people at the door of the Way. If a family is close to the Way, then the heart will be the star. Even if a man is skilled at leading the people through the passes, he will be humble. The gentleman is the strong man of the Way. When the gentleman makes the Way his mind, he will establish the Way in the mountains and forests, and the people will be able to graze their animals in the mountains and forests.
